{"step_by_step_thinking": "The relevant information comes from Document [0] of the MEDCORP source, which states 'While there are many variations, the basic steps for a subepithelial connective tissue graft after anesthetizing donor and recipient sites are described in Box 6.3. Typically, recession treatment results in significant root coverage or complete coverage in Cairo RT-1 type defects, partial gain in Cairo RT-2 type defects, and no predictable gain in Cairo RT-3 defects.' This indicates that for Cairo RT-3 defects, there is no predictable gain with recession treatment. Therefore, the placement of a graft will be failure in Class III recession.", "answer_choice": "C"}
